name: faster_rcnn

members.name: FasterRCNNConfig
members.signature: FasterRCNNConfig(num_classes: int = 80, in_channels: int = 3, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 2000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 16.0, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_size: int = 7, spatial_scale: float = 1.0 / 16.0, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, dropout: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 300)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 80, in_channels: int = 3, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 2000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 16.0, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_size: int = 7, spatial_scale: float = 1.0 / 16.0, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, dropout: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 300)

members.extended:
Faster R-CNN (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015) replaces external proposal methods
(selective search) with a learned Region Proposal Network (RPN) that shares
convolutional features with the detection head.  This makes the entire
pipeline end-to-end trainable and near real-time.

parameters.name: rpn_anchor_sizes
parameters.description:
Anchor base sizes per FPN level (single-level
                 for VGG backbone: one set of anchors).

parameters.name: rpn_pre_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per level before NMS.

parameters.name: rpn_post_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per image after NMS.

parameters.name: rpn_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Anchor→GT IoU upper bound for background.

parameters.name: spatial_scale
parameters.description: Feature-map / image scale ratio (1/16 for VGG16).

parameters.name: roi_fg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Proposal→GT IoU for fg assignment in RoI head.

parameters.name: roi_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Proposal→GT IoU upper bound for bg in RoI head.

notes:
Image → VGG16 backbone (conv1_1 … conv5_3, stride 16) → feature map
Feature map → RPN → region proposals
Feature map + proposals → RoI Pool (7×7) → FC head → cls + bbox

members.name: FasterRCNNForObjectDetection

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, targets: list[dict[str, Tensor]] | None = None)
methods.summary: Run Faster R-CNN on a batch of images.

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description:
Optional training targets (list of dicts with
     "boxes" and "labels").

returns.description:
`ObjectDetectionOutput`:
`logits`     : (Σ proposals, num_classes+1) raw class logits.
`pred_boxes` : (Σ proposals, 4) decoded xyxy boxes.
`loss`       : total loss when targets provided.

methods.name: postprocess
methods.signature: postprocess(output: ObjectDetectionOutput, proposals: list[Tensor] | None = None)
methods.summary: Per-class NMS on raw detector output.
methods.extended:
Reads per-image proposals from `output.proposals` (populated
by `forward`) when not given explicitly.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description:
Raw RoI-head outputs from `forward` — class logits,
box deltas, and (optionally) the proposals they were
evaluated on.

parameters.name: proposals
parameters.description:
Per-image `(N_i, 4)` xyxy proposal boxes.  Required when
`output.proposals` is `None`; otherwise this falls back
to the latter for the typical call-after-forward pattern.

returns.description:
One entry per image, each a dict with keys
`"boxes"` (`(K, 4)` xyxy), `"scores"` (`(K,)`), and
`"labels"` (`(K,)` int64).

members.summary: Faster R-CNN end-to-end object detector (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
members.extended:
Folds region proposal generation into the network via the Region
Proposal Network (RPN), eliminating the external selective-search
bottleneck of R-CNN / Fast R-CNN.  The RPN shares the backbone feature
map with the detection head: at every spatial location it predicts
objectness scores and bounding-box deltas for $k = |\mathrm{sizes}| \times |\mathrm{ratios}|$ anchors, applies NMS to retain the
`post_nms_top_n` highest-scoring proposals, and forwards them to
the Fast R-CNN-style RoI head for class-specific classification and
box refinement.  The pipeline is end-to-end trainable with a four-term
loss

$$
L = L^{\mathrm{rpn}}_{\mathrm{cls}} + L^{\mathrm{rpn}}_{\mathrm{loc}}
    + L^{\mathrm{det}}_{\mathrm{cls}} + L^{\mathrm{det}}_{\mathrm{loc}}.
$$

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the `faster_rcnn` factory for
the paper-cited VGG16 configuration (3-scale, 3-ratio anchors at
stride 16; 2000 / 1000 pre/post-NMS proposals at training time).

examples:
Run inference with internal proposal generation (no external proposals
required):
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.faster_rcnn import faster_rcnn
>>> model = faster_rcnn(num_classes=80)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 800, 800)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]   # K + 1
81
Training pass with ground-truth targets (computes RPN + detection
multi-task loss):
>>> targets = [{
...     "boxes":  lucid.tensor([[100.0, 100.0, 400.0, 400.0]]),
...     "labels": lucid.tensor([5], dtype=lucid.int64),
... }]
>>> out = model(x, targets=targets)
>>> out.loss.shape
()
notes:
See Ren et al., "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with
Region Proposal Networks", NeurIPS 2015 ([arXiv:1506.01497](https://arxiv.org/abs/1506.01497)).  The
Region Proposal Network performs anchor-based regression: for each
anchor $A$, the predicted offset $(t_x, t_y, t_w, t_h)$
decodes to a proposal

$$
\begin{aligned}
    \hat{G}_x &= A_w t_x + A_x, & \hat{G}_w &= A_w \exp(t_w), \\
    \hat{G}_y &= A_h t_y + A_y, & \hat{G}_h &= A_h \exp(t_h).
\end{aligned}
$$
The RPN is trained with binary cross-entropy on objectness and
smooth-$L_1$ on box deltas; anchors with IoU $\geq$
`rpn_fg_iou_thresh` are positive and IoU $<$ `rpn_bg_iou_thresh`
are negative.  Detection head losses match Fast R-CNN.  Default
`bbox_reg_weights = (10, 10, 5, 5)` matches the reference Caffe
implementation.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: backbone
attributes.description:
Shared VGG16 conv1_1 .. conv5_3 trunk producing a stride-16
feature map $(B, 512, H/16, W/16)$.

attributes.name: rpn_head
attributes.description:
3x3 conv + sibling 1x1 conv heads for objectness logits
(`B, k, fH, fW`) and per-anchor box deltas (`B, 4k, fH, fW`).

attributes.name: roi_head
attributes.description:
Fast R-CNN-style head: RoI Pool (7x7) -> `fc6` -> `fc7` ->
sibling class (K + 1) and class-specific box (4K) outputs.

attributes.name: _anchor_gen
attributes.description:
Anchor box generator with `sizes` x `ratios` boxes per cell;
produces $fH \cdot fW \cdot k$ reference boxes per image.

members.name: faster_rcnn
members.summary: Faster R-CNN with VGG16 backbone (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
members.extended:
Builds a `FasterRCNNForObjectDetection` with the paper-cited
VGG16 + 3-scale/3-ratio anchor configuration: anchors at sizes
`(128, 256, 512)` and aspect ratios `(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)` at every
stride-16 cell (9 anchors / cell), 2000 / 1000 pre / post-NMS proposals
at training time, and a 7x7 RoI Pool detection head matching Fast R-CNN.
PASCAL VOC 2007 test mAP of 73.2% with selective-search replaced by
the learned RPN (paper Table 6) and ~5 fps inference on a Titan X.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future pretrained-weight loading.  Currently ignored.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `FasterRCNNConfig` —
common knobs include `num_classes`, `rpn_anchor_sizes` /
`rpn_anchor_ratios` for different scale priors,
`rpn_post_nms_top_n` to vary the proposal budget, and
`score_thresh` / `nms_thresh` for inference filtering.

returns.description:
Detector with the Faster R-CNN VGG16 configuration applied (or
with `overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.faster_rcnn import faster_rcnn
>>> model = faster_rcnn(num_classes=80)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 800, 800)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]
81
notes:
See Ren et al., "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection
with Region Proposal Networks", NeurIPS 2015 ([arXiv:1506.01497](https://arxiv.org/abs/1506.01497)).
The key contribution is replacing the external selective-search
proposal stage with a learned RPN that shares the backbone — at the
cost of one extra 3x3 conv plus two 1x1 sibling convs per cell.

summary: Faster R-CNN — object detection (Ren et al., NeurIPS 2015).
extended:
Paper: "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with
        Region Proposal Networks"
citation: Ren, Shaoqing, et al. "Faster R-CNN: Towards Real-Time Object Detection with Region Proposal Networks." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2015.
theory:
Faster R-CNN removes the last hand-engineered component of the
R-CNN family — the external selective-search proposal step — by
introducing a **Region Proposal Network (RPN)** that *shares
convolutional features* with the detection head.

The RPN is a small fully-convolutional network that slides over
the backbone feature map.  At every spatial position it evaluates
$k$ reference boxes called **anchors** (the paper uses
$k = 9$ from 3 scales × 3 aspect ratios) and emits two
heads:

- **Objectness** — a $2k$-dim softmax classifying each
  anchor as foreground or background.
- **Box regression** — a $4k$-dim parameterised offset
  $t = (t_x, t_y, t_w, t_h)$ refining anchors toward
  ground-truth boxes.

Anchors with IoU $\ge 0.7$ against any GT are positive;
$\le 0.3$ are negative.  The multi-task loss is



$$
L(\{p_i\}, \{t_i\}) =
    \tfrac{1}{N_{\mathrm{cls}}} \sum_i L_{\mathrm{cls}}(p_i, p_i^*)
    + \lambda \, \tfrac{1}{N_{\mathrm{reg}}} \sum_i p_i^*
      \, L_{\mathrm{reg}}(t_i, t_i^*).
$$

Top-scoring proposals (after NMS) replace selective search
completely.  The **same backbone** then feeds the Fast R-CNN
head, so the entire detector is end-to-end trainable.  With a
VGG-16 trunk the whole pipeline runs at ≈5 fps while matching
or exceeding selective-search Fast R-CNN accuracy — a milestone
that established two-stage anchor-based detection as the
dominant paradigm and seeded all later FPN / Mask R-CNN / Cascade
R-CNN extensions.
